video:video.video:title: Tornado barrels through Oklahoma, damaging homes and shutting down roads
video:video.video:description: A powerful tornado in Oklahoma on Thursday ripped roofs off buildings, knocked down power poles and sent emergency crews rushing into a rural community near Vance Air Force Base, officials said.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 24
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, April 24th: Devastating tornado strikes Oklahoma; Trump orders military to ‘shoot and kill’ Iranian small boats chocking the Strait of Hormuz; Trump says Israel and Lebanon extended ceasefire; U.S. soldier charged with using classified information in online betting site

video:video.video:title: Molly Gordon's love of all things English
video:video.video:description: Molly Gordon, the star of "The Bear," is in love with all things English - especially after filming in the country for comedy mystery "The Sheep Detectives." (April 24)

video:video.video:title: Ethan Hawke's tip for staying positive through change
video:video.video:description: TIME100 honoree Ethan Hawke reacts to the Paramount-Warner Bros merger.

video:video.video:title: Man who helped runner who collapsed near Boston Marathon's finish line says he acted on instinct
video:video.video:description: Ajay Haridasse was close to finishing the Boston Marathon when his legs gave out. But as he struggled to get up, two other runners came along, lifted him up and helped him about 1,000 feet (305 meters) to the finish line.

video:video.video:title: Immersive installations, sustainability and latest trends at Milan's design week
video:video.video:description: Milan has once again become the global capital of design as Salone del Mobile and Fuorisalone take over the city.

video:video.video:title: Cautious optimism in Beirut and Israel as Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire extended
video:video.video:description: The initial 10-day ceasefire, which took effect last Friday, had been due to expire Monday. People from both Lebanon and Israel reacted on Friday to the extension of the ceasefire, which was brokered by the U.S.

video:video.video:title: How you store produce can make it last longer
video:video.video:description: How you store produce can make a major difference in how long it lasts. Factors like moisture, temperature and airflow affect how quickly food spoils.

video:video.video:title: Ewe’ve got to be kidding: It's "The Sheep Detectives"
video:video.video:description: The human stars of "The Sheep Detectives" had no real sheep to work with on their new murder mystery yarn, which sees Julia Louis-Dreyfus and Chris O'Dowd voicing two of the film's woolly stars.

video:video.video:title: US soldier charged with using classified intel to win $400K Polymarket bet on Maduro raid
video:video.video:description: Gannon Ken Van Dyke was part of the operation to capture former Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro in January and used his access to classified information to make money on the prediction market site Polymarket, the federal prosecutor’s office in New York said.

video:video.video:title: Noah Wyle's 'Baby Jane Doe' impression
video:video.video:description: Noah Wyle does his best "Baby Jane Doe" as he singles out Katherine LaNasa's performance as Dana in "The Pitt."

video:video.video:title: Meet the baby koala hiding in its mom’s pouch at a Florida zoo’s new Outback habitat
video:video.video:description: A zoo in Florida has two reasons to celebrate — the first birth of a koala and a newly renovated habitat for the cuddly creatures is opening to the public.

video:video.video:title: How do tornadoes form and why are they so common in the US?
video:video.video:description: There are more tornadoes in the US than anywhere else on Earth. How do they form? This is in part because of the geography of the US.

video:video.video:title: AI smart glasses to help visually impaired runners take on London Marathon
video:video.video:description: Visually impaired runners are using AI powered smart glasses as they prepare for the London Marathon.

video:video.video:title: Violent tornado tears through Oklahoma town, damaging 40 homes but sparing lives
video:video.video:description: People in Enid began cleaning up Friday after a powerful storm churned up at least one violent tornado that barreled through the town. (AP video: Sean Murphy)

video:video.video:title: Activists attempt to take dogs from Wisconsin facility
video:video.video:description: About 1,000 animal welfare activists tried to gain entry again this month to Ridglan Farms, a beagle breeding and research facility in Wisconsin, before they were turned back by police who fired rubber bullets and pepper spray.

video:video.video:title: EU joined by Middle Eastern leaders at summit with US-Iran war energy crisis top of the agenda
video:video.video:description: The European Union summit in Cyprus was joined by Middle Eastern leaders on Friday to discuss their response to the energy crisis brought by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Wounded Lebanese journalist recounts Israeli attacks which killed her colleague from hospital bed
video:video.video:description: A Lebanese journalist who was wounded in an Israeli airstrike that killed her colleague this week described hours of agony as they waited for help to arrive.

video:video.video:title: After 42 years, the Miss Indian World pageant hangs up its crown
video:video.video:description: It’s the final year of an event that bills itself as the largest powwow in North America. The last Gathering of Nations kicked off on Friday in New Mexico.

video:video.video:title: US says Strait of Hormuz blockade expanding, details attack on Iranian vessel
video:video.video:description: U.S. Defense Secretary Hegseth says the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz will expand with a second aircraft carrier arriving in Gulf. Hegseth was joined by Gen.

video:video.video:title: Peru police raid ex-election chief’s home as ballot shortages spark widening probe
video:video.video:description: Police in Peru raided the homes Friday of the now-resigned elections chief and five other officials, in an investigation into ballot shortages and other irregularities in the first round of the presidential election on April 12.

video:video.video:title: Ex-funeral home owner gets 30 years in Colorado corpse abuse case
video:video.video:description: A former Colorado funeral home owner who helped her ex-husband hide nearly 200 decomposing bodies was sentenced to 30 years in prison Friday, in a case that forced the state to clamp down on an industry plagued by repeated scandal and notoriously lax oversight.

video:video.video:title: Iranian foreign minister lands in Pakistan for meetings ahead of expected talks with US envoys
video:video.video:description: Abbas Araghchi in Islamabad for negotiations with U.S. envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner. Acting as a mediator, Pakistan has been trying to get the United States and Iran to a second round of ceasefire negotiations.

video:video.video:title: NYC's new corrections boss once served time in a jail he now oversees.
video:video.video:description: New York City’s new correction commissioner, Stanley Richards, once served more than two years at the notorious Rikers Island jail before dedicating his career to helping inmates turnaround their lives.

video:video.video:title: Palestinian couples celebrate mass wedding in Gaza
video:video.video:description: The celebration of 150 couples, which was funded by Al Fares Al Shahim, a humanitarian aid operation backed by the United Arab Emirates, was a rare glimpse of hope and resilience displayed by Palestinians whose lives have been shattered by two years of war.

video:video.video:title: Tick season seems to be off to a fast start
video:video.video:description: Tick season seems to be off to a fast start, with an unusually high number of bites already reported across the country.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: King Charles on mission to restore UK-US relationship in Washington
video:video.video:description: State visits are always carried out at the request of the British government to cement international relationships.

video:video.video:title: In Rhode Island, nonprofit works to increase tree equity
video:video.video:description: Newport, Rhode Island, has neighborhoods of opulent mansions with lots of mature trees along with neighborhoods of subsidized housing and far fewer trees.

video:video.video:title: Philadelphia Museum of Art embraces Rocky Balboa statue's worldwide appeal
video:video.video:description: A new exhibition, “Rising Up: Rocky and the Making of Monuments,” brings the bronze figure of Rocky Balboa inside for the first time, reframing it as both pop culture icon and part of a 2,000-year tradition of boxing imagery.

video:video.video:title: AP sees inside Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant 40 years after disaster
video:video.video:description: The two explosions at the Chernobyl nuclear power plant came decades apart in the dead of night. The first, at 1:23 a.m. on April 26, 1986, spread a cloud of deadly radiation that raised fears across Europe and shook the very foundations of the Soviet Union.

video:video.video:title: Japan wakes up to Radio Taiso exercise tradition
video:video.video:description: This is how Japan wakes up. It's called Radio Taiso — Exercise Radio in English — a simple yet dynamic way to start the day.

video:video.video:title: Oklahoma governor tours rural town damaged by powerful tornado
video:video.video:description: Oklahoma Gov. Kevin Stitt toured the small rural town of Enid Friday, about 85 miles (about 135 kilometres) north of Oklahoma City, where an EF-4 tornado hit on Thursday night.

video:video.video:title: Overnight attack in Dnipro injures at least 14, emergency services say
video:video.video:description: At least 14 people, including one child, have been injured as a result of an overnight attack in Ukraine's Dnipro, State Emergency Service of Ukraine said Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Bears shake off winter with a splash at Oregon Zoo
video:video.video:description: Two young black bears at the Oregon Zoo in Portland are welcoming spring with splashes, sparring and sun-soaked play after months of winter dormancy.

video:video.video:title: Flight canceled amid fuel shortages? Here’s what to do
video:video.video:description: Flight canceled due to fuel shortages? Here’s what to do if your plans are disrupted.

video:video.video:title: Prince Harry speaks on legacy of Ukraine war, hopes global community can 'speak truth to power'
video:video.video:description: Britain's Prince Harry said on Friday that the legacy of any war is "huge" and that it's important to bring an end to global conflicts "sooner rather than later" during his third visit in a year to Ukraine.

video:video.video:title: Police identify the body of 1 missing USF student, second still missing as roommate is charged
video:video.video:description: Hillsborough County Sheriff Chad Chronister said Zamil Limon’s remains were found on the Howard Frankland bridge Friday morning, but Nahida Bristy is still missing.

video:video.video:title: Students building high-powered rockets get inspired by Artemis II mission
video:video.video:description: Students who built and launched their own high-powered rockets as a part of a NASA educational program said the recent Artemis II mission has inspired them.

video:video.video:title: Oklahoma Gov. Stitt says he's thankful no lives were lost after a tornado hit the state
video:video.video:description: Oklahoma Gov. Kevin Stitt said Friday he was thankful no one died after a powerful tornado that barreled through the rural town of Enid, Oklahoma, on Thursday night.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 24
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, April 24th: Wildfires and storms displace residents in Georgia, Oklahoma; DOJ ends criminal probe of Fed Chair Jerome Powell; U.S.

video:video.video:title: World Cup final tickets listed on FIFA resale at over $2 million
video:video.video:description: FIFA’s resale site has four tickets on sale for the World Cup final for just under $2.3 million each. (AP Production: Marissa Duhaney)

video:video.video:title: Women demanding release of political prisoners lead fight for civil liberties in Venezuela
video:video.video:description: Venezuelan women have spent the year protesting to demand the release of hundreds of political prisoners.

video:video.video:title: Georgia wildfires destroy over 120 homes, threaten nearly 1,000 more
video:video.video:description: Two large wildfires burning in drought-stricken southeast Georgia have destroyed more than 120 homes and are threatening nearly 1,000 more. (AP video: Emilie Megnien)

video:video.video:title: US military strike on alleged drug boat kills 2 in eastern Pacific
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said it launched another strike Friday on a boat accused of ferrying drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing two people.

video:video.video:title: Kate, Princess of Wales, lays wreath at London's Cenotaph on Anzac Day
video:video.video:description: Kate, the Princess of Wales, laid a wreath on behalf of King Charles III at the Cenotaph in London on Sunday to mark Anzac Day, which commemorates Australians and New Zealanders who died in conflict.

video:video.video:title: Russian drone attack rekindles Ukrainians' memories of 1986 Chernobyl nuclear disaster
video:video.video:description: The Russian drone hit the outer layer of what is known as the New Safe Confinement structure, or NSC, the vast, $2.1 billion arch-like shell that was completed in 2019 to enclose the original, hastily built concrete “sarcophagus” to keep the damaged Reactor No. 4 and its deadly debris from leaking r

video:video.video:title: Anti-war demonstrators in Madrid back Sánchez in confrontation with Trump over war
video:video.video:description: A Pentagon email was recently leaked suggesting that Spain should be expelled from NATO for failing to cooperate with the United States in its war against Iran.

video:video.video:title: Iranian FM Araghchi meets Pakistani PM Sharif
video:video.video:description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrived in Pakistan's capital of Islamabad on Friday to take part in the Pakistani-led mediation efforts, and held a series of meetings with Pakistani officials.

video:video.video:title: 'Live our lives for three seconds': Palestinians mourn relatives killed in Israeli airstrikes
video:video.video:description: They were the latest of the almost 800 people killed in Gaza since a fragile ceasefire between Hamas and Israel was put in place last October, according to figures of Hamas-run Gaza’s health ministry.

video:video.video:title: Militants seen moving outside Mali capital amid reports of attacks
video:video.video:description: Islamic militants and separatists attacked several locations in Mali's capital and other cities on Saturday, in one of the largest coordinated attacks in the country in recent years.

video:video.video:title: Faster than Bolt: Corgis race for glory in Prague
video:video.video:description: Corgi dogs were racing against each other in an Olympic-style event in Prague in the Czech Republic on Saturday. The fastest record was 8.9 seconds for a 100-metre race.

video:video.video:title: Roommate charged with two counts of murder in death, disappearance of two USF students
video:video.video:description: Authorities filed murder charges against the roommate of a Bangladeshi doctoral student who disappeared with his girlfriend from the University of South Florida.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 25
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, April 25th: Trump called off dispatching envoys in Iran talks; Roommate of missing USF student is charged with two counts of murder; Zelenskyy calls for more talks after Russian strikes kill five in Ukraine; Rocky exhibit debuts in Philadelphia.

video:video.video:title: Lisbon parade celebrates anniversary of Portugal's Carnation Revolution
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of people marched along Lisbon’s Freedom Avenue on Saturday to celebrate the anniversary of the Carnation Revolution.

video:video.video:title: Unmanned cargo spacecraft takes off for International Space Station
video:video.video:description: The unmanned Progress 95 took off on board a Soyuz rocket from the Baikonur Cosmodrome. The Roscosmos spacecraft carries food, fuel, and supplies for the crew on board the International Space Station.

video:video.video:title: Online businesses struggle as Iran keeps internet shutdown in place
video:video.video:description: As Amen Khademi applies lipstick to her model before a photo shoot, her mind is occupied with thoughts of how to keep her business alive.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians vote in local elections in West Bank and part of Gaza
video:video.video:description: Palestinians have voted in local elections, the first in two decades in Gaza and the first in the Israeli occupied West Bank since the start of the war in Gaza.

video:video.video:title: Police say Chicago hospital shooting left one officer dead and another critically injured
video:video.video:description: A police officer died and another was critically injured after a shooting at a Chicago hospital on Saturday, according to officials.

video:video.video:title: Correspondents arrive at the White House after Trump announces news conference
video:video.video:description: Dozens of journalists arrived at the White House after President Donald Trump announced a news conference following a shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner.

video:video.video:title: Trump gives statement after security incident at White House Correspondents' dinner
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump addressed the media at the White House, after he, the First Lady and senior U.S. leaders were evacuated from an annual dinner of White House correspondents on Saturday night after a security incident.

video:video.video:title: New supersized telescope in Atacama desert could offer view of Earth-like planets
video:video.video:description: In Chile’s Atacama Desert, the night sky feels infinite. Considered the driest place on Earth, its darkness offers one of the clearest windows to the universe.

video:video.video:title: 'Everbody's safe': Head of White House correspondents' association says after security incident
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump and other leaders were unhurt in the security incident at the annual White House correspondents' dinner on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran presented new offers 10 minutes after he canceled US team’s trip
video:video.video:description: The latest ceasefire talks between the United States and Iran appeared to fail Saturday before they began, as Tehran's top diplomat left Pakistan and President Donald Trump soon afterward said he had told envoys not to travel to Islamabad.

video:video.video:title: Security video shows suspect running toward White House correspondents' dinner
video:video.video:description: Security video appears to show suspect running towards White House correspondents' dinner

video:video.video:title: Deadly blast leaves chaos on highway as violence escalates in southwestern Colombia
video:video.video:description: At least 14 people were killed when an explosive device detonated as a bus passed by on a highway in southwestern Colombia on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: First-hand account of Trump's evacuation after security incident at correspondents' dinner
video:video.video:description: First-hand account of Trump's evacuation after security incident at correspondents' dinner

video:video.video:title: Trump gives statement after security incident at White House Correspondents' dinner
video:video.video:description: He addressed the media at the White House, after he, the First Lady and senior U.S. leaders were evacuated from an annual dinner of White House correspondents on Saturday night after a security incident.

video:video.video:title: WATCH: Law enforcement agents escort President Trump after security incident
video:video.video:description: Incident at White House Correspondents' Dinner prompts security response

video:video.video:title: Trump says being president is a ‘dangerous profession’ after correspondents' dinner shooting
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump struck a somber tone as he addressed reporters at the White House, saying being president is “a dangerous profession” and that attempted violence against him is “part of the job.”
